title: Roasted Rich Ethiopian Beets Gomen
description: A funky and kid-friendly Ethiopian dish featuring roasted beets, this recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner. It is also Buddhist-temple-friendly and can be served as a side dish or main course.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the chopped beets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the beets out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
While the beets are roasting, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Once the beets have finished roasting, add them to the skillet with the onion mixture.
Stir in the berbere spice and cook for 1 minute.
Serve the Roasted Rich Ethiopian Beets Gomen hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
